q: Optics is the branch of physics that studies the nature and properties of light.
A laser is an acronym for Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation.
Lasers can only produce light in the visible spectrum.
The laser medium can be a gas, liquid, or semiconductor crystal.
The power supply in a laser system is used for the pumping process.
The optical cavity consists of two mirrors with 100% reflectivity.
Population inversion is an essential process to initiate a laser action.
During stimulated emission, the radiated photons have different frequencies.
The output laser beam is characterized as being coherent.
A $1 \text{ mW}$ He-Ne laser beam is much dimmer than sunlight.
Lasers are used to destroy kidney stones.
